summary_one_line: A DTD element content model with two or more children joined by a ',' or '|' separator, so the loop sets both cur and last.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must drive parsing deep into a DTD <!ELEMENT (...) content declaration where xmlParseElementChildrenContentDeclPriv parses a first element, then takes the while-loop body on a ',' or '|' separator to parse a SECOND element, so that both cur and last are non-NULL at line 6513.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed contains a parenthesized content group with element names and an actual separator path: the W overlay hits the '|' branch at 6418 and the second-child parsing at 6484-6508, and the per-role DIVERGENT BRANCHES show W taking the true side of the 6513 blocker (T=6 F=0 and T=3 F=3) where L never does.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds stall in DTD/external-subset setup (<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...") and either never reach the content loop or exit it after a single element: at 6513 the loser is T=0 F=65, i.e. it always takes the false branch because last stays NULL.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) on top of the I2S that cmplog already has; this gradient rewards partial progress on the long multi-byte literal comparisons (e.g. the #PCDATA CMP7 and DOCTYPE/encoding string compares on the path), letting vpc assemble the structurally deeper multi-child content model that enters the loop a second time, whereas cmplog (I2S only, same as the winner) lacks the gradient and cannot build that depth.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires both cur and last to be non-NULL at line 6513.
last is only assigned inside the while loop after a separator and a second element are parsed.
The winner takes the '|' separator branch of the content loop that the loser never enters.
At the blocker the winner takes the true side while the loser only ever takes the false side (last NULL).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
if ((cur != NULL) && (last != NULL)) {  <-- BLOCKER
  d=1   L6515  T=3 F=0

evidence_trail.claim: The loser-blocking seeds are stuck in external-subset DTD setup rather than a deep content model.
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the per-execution CMP buffer that cmplog also has.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both the winner and the loser carry I2SRandReplace, so I2S is not the differentiating technique.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6654 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 2 --seed ae2d760d094b65d2`; the chain shows I2S-floor dash rows at depth 5 and 6, but the decisive pair delta is value_profile (both vpc AND cmplog have I2SRandReplace), so the dash rows do NOT explain the divergence. The differentiator is the CMP_MAP gradient feedback unique to value_profile_cmplog, which lineage does not directly tag; thus the value_profile mechanism could not be confirmed via dash-row lineage and rests on source/structure plus the union-instrumentation note.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating line 6513's true side behind a single shallow multi-byte literal compare (no nested/structural depth) where cmplog reaches the true side as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing the gradient confers no advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The attribution to CMP_MAP gradient is inferential: the prompt confirms vpc has the gradient and cmplog does not, but I could not directly observe a gradient-driven find in lineage (dash rows present are I2S, shared by both fuzzers).
